# 15 Best Customer Support Software for B2B in 2026 (Compared)

## Summary

Plain's own blog publishes a quarterly-updated comparison of 15 B2B customer support platforms, drawing on "619 conversations with support leaders, technical founders, and customer engineers." Published 2026-03-13. While Plain-authored and therefore biased toward Plain, it contains the most comprehensive pricing table and feature comparison across all major platforms in a single document.

**Top platforms ranked by use case:**

1. **Plain** -- Best overall for B2B SaaS (API-first, GraphQL, BYOA, Slack/Teams/Discord native, $39/seat/mo)
2. **Zendesk** -- Best for large enterprise (complex routing, voice, extensive integrations)
3. **Intercom** -- Best for product-led SaaS with in-app chat volume
4. **Help Scout** -- Documentation-driven, email-first, simplicity-first teams
5. **Front** -- Collaborative shared inbox for omnichannel
6. **Pylon** -- Best for Slack-heavy B2B teams ($59/seat/mo, Slack Connect focus)
7. **Freshdesk** -- SMB / budget-accessible (free tier for 10 agents)

**Plain's stated differentiators (2026):**

- GraphQL API with full internal parity and no rate limits -- only platform with this
- Bring Your Own Agent (BYOA): deploy any LLM as the support agent on Plain's infrastructure
- Native first-class channels: Slack, Teams, Discord, email (not bolt-on integrations)
- Auto-triage at ~92% accuracy for categorization and routing
- Ari AI agent and Sidekick copilot included on every plan
- Cursor Lookup: connects support directly to codebases for technical answers
- Starting price: $39/seat/mo (updated from earlier $35 -- appears to have changed in Q1 2026)

**Pylon (from same article):**

- Pricing: $59/seat/mo Starter, $89/seat/mo Professional
- Customers: Deel, Hightouch, Merge, Temporal, AssemblyAI
- Strengths: Strong Slack Connect, account management for CS teams, AI agents for automated response
- Limitations: API lags API-first platforms, higher starting price, reliability issues with Slack ticketing reported by some users

**Intercom (from same article):**

- Best for product-led SaaS with high in-app chat volume
- Traditional ticket-based workflow; less suitable for engineering-led B2B

## Key takeaways

- The developer-tools category (Vercel, Cursor, n8n, Raycast, Stytch) has concentrated on Plain -- this is the strongest signal for a B2B API-first product recommending Plain
- Plain's price appears to have moved to $39/seat/mo in 2026 (some sources still cite $35); confirm current pricing at plain.com/pricing before recommending
- Pylon's position at #8 in a Plain-authored list is still fairly high; their Slack-native differentiation is acknowledged even by a competitor
- The B2B support tool market has bifurcated: API-first (Plain) vs. operations-first (Pylon, Intercom, Zendesk)
- No single tool covers all use cases: choose based on team type (engineering-led vs. ops-led), channel mix (Slack-centric vs. email-centric), and scale
